evals.name: fastapi-no-tests-add-suite
evals.prompt: I've got a FastAPI service at plugins/quality-assurance/skills/smoke-test/evals/fixtures/fastapi-app with zero tests. Can you add a smoke suite for it?

assertions.text: Proposes a focused list of 5-10 concrete checks before generating any code
Generated tests target the actual routes that exist in the fixture (e.g. /health, /users/{id}, /orders) rather than invented endpoints
Output uses a Python-native test framework appropriate for FastAPI (pytest + httpx/TestClient or similar) rather than a generic curl script
Marks environment-specific values like base URL or auth tokens with a clear TODO/placeholder rather than hardcoding production values

evals.name: express-deploy-pain
evals.prompt: We keep wasting half our mornings on broken deploys. This is the repo: plugins/quality-assurance/skills/smoke-test/evals/fixtures/express-app. I want a fast test that proves the deploy isn't completely dead before QA touches it.

assertions.text: Recognises this is a Node/Express project and picks a JS-native runner (the fixture already has vitest/supertest in devDeps — should be used)
Keeps the suite small and explicitly fast — caps total runtime around 2 minutes or fewer than ~10 checks
Checks include at least one auth-or-error path, not only happy 200s, so a broken deploy actually surfaces

evals.name: go-ci-wiring
evals.prompt: Need build-verification tests wired into GitHub Actions for the Go HTTP API in plugins/quality-assurance/skills/smoke-test/evals/fixtures/go-http-app. Should run on every PR and block merge if anything is red.

assertions.text: Produces both the Go test code and a GitHub Actions workflow YAML wiring them in
The Go tests use the standard testing package (or a standard idiom for Go HTTP services) rather than a foreign-language runner
Workflow is configured so failures actually block the PR (sets up Go, runs the smoke target, exits non-zero on failure)

evals.name: axum-post-deploy-checks
evals.prompt: I want post-deploy health checks for our axum service before it promotes to prod. Repo is at plugins/quality-assurance/skills/smoke-test/evals/fixtures/axum-app. Idempotent please — they'll run against the live env.

assertions.text: The proposed checks are explicitly idempotent — no side-effectful POSTs that mutate prod data without cleanup
Output is in Rust (uses reqwest/cargo test or an equivalent Rust-native runner), not a script in another language
Distinguishes liveness/readiness from deeper route checks so the suite stays meaningful against a real deployed env

evals.name: pick-which-routes
evals.prompt: Help me pick which 5-10 routes our smoke suite should hit. Service is the FastAPI one in plugins/quality-assurance/skills/smoke-test/evals/fixtures/fastapi-app.

assertions.text: Returns a prioritised list of routes with a one-line justification each, not just a code dump
Justifications reflect smoke-test discipline — critical-path coverage, blast radius, auth surface — not exhaustive coverage
Total count stays within the 5-10 bound the user asked for

evals.name: bvt-jargon
evals.prompt: set up a BVT for plugins/quality-assurance/skills/smoke-test/evals/fixtures/express-app

assertions.text: Treats 'BVT' as build-verification testing — i.e. recognises it as a smoke-test request rather than asking what BVT means
Produces a runnable scaffold for the Express fixture, not just a definition of the term

evals.name: review-cut-for-speed
evals.prompt: Our existing smoke suite for the FastAPI service at plugins/quality-assurance/skills/smoke-test/evals/fixtures/fastapi-app takes 7 minutes. What should I cut?

assertions.text: Asks to see (or reads) the existing suite before proposing cuts rather than guessing
Recommendations explicitly target the <2-minute / 5-10-check discipline and explain which checks duplicate coverage or belong in a deeper tier
Does not propose generating a brand-new suite from scratch when the user only asked for a review

evals.name: intake-testing-phrasing
evals.prompt: I need an intake testing layer for the Go service in plugins/quality-assurance/skills/smoke-test/evals/fixtures/go-http-app — something the deploy pipeline runs first to decide whether the rest of the suite is even worth executing.

assertions.text: Recognises 'intake testing' as smoke / build-acceptance and produces a smoke scaffold (does not ask for clarification)
Output is genuinely small and fast — designed as a gate, not a full integration suite

evals.name: sanity-vs-smoke
evals.prompt: What's the difference between sanity tests and smoke tests, and which one do I want for the axum app at plugins/quality-assurance/skills/smoke-test/evals/fixtures/axum-app?

assertions.text: Gives a clear short definition distinguishing the two, then recommends which one fits the user's situation
If a smoke suite is recommended, offers to scaffold one for the axum fixture rather than ending at theory

evals.name: stressed-quick-suite
evals.prompt: ship is on fire. need a smoke suite for the express app in plugins/quality-assurance/skills/smoke-test/evals/fixtures/express-app. fast.

assertions.text: Responds without excessive preamble — gets to a proposed check list or scaffold quickly
Still surfaces the 5-10 proposed checks before writing code, rather than silently generating a huge file
Final scaffold is actually runnable against the fixture (correct framework wiring, no obvious import errors)

evals.name: adversarial-unit-tests
evals.prompt: Can you write unit tests for the order-pricing function in my checkout module? I want full branch coverage and mocks for the tax service.

assertions.text: Does not produce a smoke/BVT scaffold — recognises this as a unit-testing request
Either declines to invoke smoke-test workflow or explicitly says smoke tests are not the right tool here

evals.name: adversarial-regression-suite
evals.prompt: Design a regression test suite for our payments service so we catch any behaviour change between releases.

assertions.text: Does not propose a 5-10-check smoke suite as the answer — recognises regression testing is a different tier
If it mentions smoke tests at all, clearly distinguishes them from regression and does not conflate the two

evals.name: adversarial-hardware-smoke
evals.prompt: Help me write a smoke test for this STM32 board — basically a quick power-on self-check to confirm the peripherals are alive.

assertions.text: Does not produce HTTP/API smoke scaffolds for a web framework — the request is about hardware
Either declines, redirects, or explicitly notes this skill is scoped to web services / APIs

evals.name: adversarial-load-test
evals.prompt: I need to load test our API and see how it behaves at 5k RPS. Can you set up a k6 or locust script?

assertions.text: Does not output a 5-10-check smoke-test suite — recognises load testing is a different discipline
Response addresses load testing (or says it's out of scope) rather than silently substituting a smoke scaffold

evals.name: adversarial-debug-single-test
evals.prompt: test_create_order keeps flaking in CI — fails maybe 1 in 5 runs. Help me debug it.

assertions.text: Engages with the debugging request rather than proposing a new smoke suite
Does not redirect into 'let me design your build-verification tests' — stays on the actual flake

evals.name: adversarial-test-pyramid
evals.prompt: Can you explain how the test pyramid applies to a typical microservices setup? Trying to plan our overall testing strategy.

assertions.text: Answers conceptually about the pyramid without jumping into scaffolding a smoke suite
Does not produce framework-specific test files for a hypothetical fixture

evals.name: edge-bash-cli
evals.prompt: I have a CLI tool written in pure Bash — about 400 lines, exposes a few subcommands. Can you add smoke tests for it?

assertions.text: Acknowledges the project isn't an HTTP service / API and adapts honestly — either offers a CLI-flavoured smoke approach (e.g. bats / shell invocations) or clearly states the skill targets web services and what the user can do instead
Does not silently generate FastAPI/Express scaffolding for a Bash project

evals.name: edge-existing-suite-review
evals.prompt: Our smoke tests live in tests/smoke/ already. Can you review them and tell me what to change? Don't generate a new suite, I want a review.

assertions.text: Treats this as a review task and asks for / reads the existing files before commenting
Does not overwrite or regenerate the existing suite when the user explicitly said review-only
Recommendations reference the project's 5-10-check / <2-minute discipline rather than generic test-writing advice

evals.name: edge-django-not-listed
evals.prompt: Add a smoke suite to our Django service. It's a standard Django REST Framework app with about 30 endpoints.

assertions.text: Does not refuse just because Django isn't in the FastAPI/Express/Go/Rust list — adapts to a Django-appropriate setup (e.g. pytest-django, Django test client, or honest generic Python HTTP smoke)
If the skill falls back to a generic template, it says so honestly rather than claiming Django-specific support it doesn't have
Still applies the 5-10-check discipline rather than trying to smoke-test all 30 endpoints

evals.name: edge-fastify-not-listed
evals.prompt: Wire up build-verification tests for our Fastify API. It's similar shape to Express but uses Fastify's plugin model.

assertions.text: Picks a Fastify-appropriate or at minimum Node-appropriate test approach (fastify.inject, supertest, or similar) rather than insisting on Express idioms
Is explicit if it is reusing an Express-style template as a stand-in, instead of pretending it's tailored to Fastify

evals.name: edge-very-large-repo
evals.prompt: Big monorepo here — 1200+ files, several services living under services/*. Need smoke tests for the orders service specifically (services/orders, it's a FastAPI app). Don't crawl the whole repo, it'll take forever.

assertions.text: Scopes its work to the named service path rather than scanning the full monorepo
Asks the user to confirm the target path, or scopes detection to it, instead of silently walking 1200+ files
Eventual scaffold is FastAPI-shaped and lives under (or targets) the orders service path

evals.name: edge-smoke-plus-auth-bug
evals.prompt: Two things — please add a smoke suite to the Express app at plugins/quality-assurance/skills/smoke-test/evals/fixtures/express-app, and while you're in there fix the auth middleware bug where it lets requests through with an expired token.

assertions.text: Handles the smoke-test request properly (proposed checks + scaffold) without ignoring it
Treats the auth bug as a separate concern — either flags it as out of scope for this skill, asks to handle it separately, or clearly separates the two pieces of work rather than tangling them
Does not silently expand the smoke suite into a full security test pass to 'cover' the auth fix
